dsl_rule.translation_notes: Condition 1: Price above long-term MA (sma_200) ensures stocks above long-term moving average. Condition 2 & 3: returns between -5% and 0% approximates 'small intraday drop' vs 'large drop'. Exit uses sma_20 crossover as reversal confirmation when price recovers. Author did not specify parameters (lookback, threshold, half_life, sharpe, backtest_period) so reasonable defaults applied: 5-day hold window, 2% stop-loss. The 'liquidity-driven vs news-driven' distinction and 'long-only fund selling pressure' cannot be directly encoded with allowed indicators.

backtest_rule.reason: Condition 1: Price above long-term MA (sma_200) ensures stocks above long-term moving average. Condition 2 & 3: returns between -5% and 0% approximates 'small intraday drop' vs 'large drop'. Exit uses sma_20 crossover as reversal confirmation when price recovers. Author did not specify parameters (lookback, threshold, half_life, sharpe, backtest_period) so reasonable defaults applied: 5-day hold window, 2% stop-loss. The 'liquidity-driven vs news-driven' distinction and 'long-only fund selling pressure' cannot be directly encoded with allowed indicators.

source_rule_text: Buy stocks that have experienced small intraday drops from their long-term moving average, as small reversions are more likely than large ones caused by fundamental news.
